title: Secondatia ferruginea
taxonomy: Secondatia ferruginea is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to tropical Africa. The classification of Secondatia ferruginea is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, the clade Eudicots, the clade Asterids, the order Gentianales, the family Rubiaceae, the tribe Cinchoneae, and the genus Secondatia. Within its genus, it is the only species currently recognized. Further research may be needed to confirm its classification.

Its extracts have been studied for their ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. The plant is also believed to have antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. In addition, Secondatia ferruginea has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever and digestive issues. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Secondatia ferruginea.
